CREATE UNLOGGED TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema1.table1 (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL);
CREATE UNLOGGED TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema2.table2 (user_id INT REFERENCES schema1.table1(id), email VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE);
CREATE UNLOGGED TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema3.table3 (order_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, user_id INT NOT NULL, amount DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL CHECK (amount > 0));
CREATE UNLOGGED TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema4.table4 (data XML NOT NULL, ts TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE UNLOGGED TABLE IF NOT EXISTS table5 (field1 INT, field2 VARCHAR(50), field3 BOOLEAN CHECK (field3 IN (TRUE, FALSE)));
CREATE UNLOGGED TABLE IF NOT EXISTS table6 (col1 INT, col2 INT, CONSTRAINT col1_col2_unique UNIQUE (col1, col2));
CREATE UNLOGGED TABLE IF NOT EXISTS table7 (description TEXT DEFAULT 'No description', created_at DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE);
CREATE UNLOGGED TABLE IF NOT EXISTS table8 (id INT CHECK (id >= 0), name VARCHAR(100), CHECK (name != ''));
CREATE UNLOGGED TABLE IF NOT EXISTS table9 (metadata JSON, version INT DEFAULT 1, CONSTRAINT version_positive CHECK (version > 0));
CREATE UNLOGGED TABLE IF NOT EXISTS table10 (user_id INT, admin BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE, PRIMARY KEY (user_id, admin));
CREATE UNLOGGED TABLE IF NOT EXISTS table11 (item_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, item_name VARCHAR(100), price DECIMAL(10, 2), quantity INT CHECK (quantity >= 0));
CREATE UNLOGGED TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema5.table12 (id INT PRIMARY KEY, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE UNLOGGED TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transactions (trans_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, amount DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL CHECK (amount >= 0), trans_timestamp TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE UNLOGGED TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_sessions (session_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, user_id INT NOT NULL, session_data TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE UNLOGGED TABLE IF NOT EXISTS complex_table (a INT, b INT, FOREIGN KEY (a) REFERENCES table1(id), FOREIGN KEY (b) REFERENCES table2(user_id));
